reproduction_targets.target: Find every exact occurrence of a pattern in a text by comparing from the pattern tail and skipping alignments
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Matches Python's reference substring search on fixed and randomized cases and uses fewer comparisons than naive search on representative long texts

datasets.name: Synthetic strings
datasets.substitute_strategy: Use deterministic and randomized texts because the paper's original test corpora are not bundled

experiments.name: Synthetic exact-match comparison
experiments.purpose: Compare Boyer-Moore-Horspool tail matching against naive search

hyperparameters.name: alphabet
hyperparameters.impact: Controls skip behavior in randomized tests

assumptions: Implement the bad-character tail-skip Horspool simplification as a Level 2 reproduction of the main skip-search idea.
Use deterministic synthetic corpora to measure comparisons.
Do not claim the original full good-suffix table implementation is reproduced.

missing_but_required.item: Original benchmark corpora and exact static tables from the 1977 paper
missing_but_required.impact: Blocks strict experimental reproduction and full original-table reproduction

modules.name: boyer_moore
modules.description: Boyer-Moore-Horspool style exact string search with comparison counting
